description: Any systemic agent causing bone marrow suppression or cytopenias; includes cytotoxic chemotherapy, targeted agents and immunological therapies with significant myelotoxicity
Class of cyclin-dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitors (abemaciclib, palbociclib, ribociclib)
Anti-vascular endothelial growth factor monoclonal antibodies, VEGFR-targeted TKIs and multi-kinase inhibitors with anti-VEGF activity
PD-1, PD-L1 and CTLA-4 inhibitor monoclonal antibodies
ADCs targeting HER2 (trastuzumab deruxtecan, trastuzumab emtansine)
Kinase inhibitors targeting EGFR, ALK and ROS1 (TKIs)
Conventional cytotoxic agents used in chemoradiation protocols
Agents with established mucosal radiosensitisation: platinum agents, fluoropyrimidines, gemcitabine, mitomycin, and EGFR-targeted agents used in mucosal field chemoradiation
Agents with established independent risk of interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis overlapping with thoracic RT
Agents with significant diarrhoea or colitis risk that overlaps with bowel irradiation

canonical_name: Bispecific T-cell engager or CAR-T therapy
description: T-cell-engaging bispecific antibodies (blinatumomab, epcoritamab, glofitamab, mosunetuzumab) and CAR-T therapies with CRS/ICANS potential

description: Agents that impair angiogenesis or wound healing, primarily anti-VEGF agents and multi-kinase inhibitors with anti-VEGF activity
